videos.title: COTA Explained: GameBot’s Low-Latency FPS Bots
videos.zhTitle: COTA Explained: GameBot’s Low-Latency FPS Bots
videos.description: COTA is a technical demo showcasing FPS bots powered by large language models (LLMs). Built in Unreal Engine and inspired by Counter-Strike, the bots are powered by a dual-layer AI system capable of macro strategy and real-time decision-making in under 100ms.

videos.title: How the Joke has Changed | Comedy in Games 2026 | Extra Credits Gaming
videos.zhTitle: How the Joke has Changed | Comedy in Games 2026 | Extra Credits Gaming
videos.description: In this episode, we break down how comedy works in games, from classic written humor like Portal, and The Stanley Parable to gameplay-driven chaos like What the Golf?, Octodad, Gang Beasts, and PEAK. We explore how structure, expectations, failure, and lack of control turn gameplay itself into the punchline.

videos.title: Video Game Difficulty is Hard (But It Doesn't Have To Be) | Extra Credits Gaming
videos.zhTitle: Video Game Difficulty is Hard (But It Doesn't Have To Be) | Extra Credits Gaming
videos.description: Difficulty settings are one of the most controversial tools in game design, and they might be solving the wrong problem. In this episode, we look at where difficulty settings came from, why they often fail players, and how modern games use adaptive systems to balance challenge, pacing, and enjoyment without forcing players to make impossible choices at the start of a game.

videos.title: MLMove: The LLM Trained to Play Counter-Strike | AI and Games #81
videos.zhTitle: MLMove: The LLM Trained to Play Counter-Strike | AI and Games #81
videos.description: For this episode of @AIandGames, Tommy sits down with David Durst: a Stanford PhD graduate to discuss his research: the 'language model of movement'.  A transformer (like those used in LLMs) that has learned to move teams of bots like humans in Counter-Strike.  By training against a dataset of retakes on Dust 2, MLMove hugs walls for cover, covers sightlines and coordinates strategy like a regular human player.
videos.searchText: mlmove: the llm trained to play counter-strike | ai and games #81 mlmove: the llm trained to play counter-strike | ai and games #81 for this episode of @aiandgames, tommy sits down with david durst: a stanford phd graduate to discuss his research: the 'language model of movement'.  a transformer (like those used in llms) that has learned to move teams of bots like humans in counter-strike.  by training against a dataset of retakes on dust 2, mlmove hugs walls for cover, covers sightlines and coordinates strategy like a regular human player. ai and games @aiandgames

videos.zhTitle: Evolving Worlds from Chaos: The Art-Led Approach of 'Darkest Dungeon 2's' Procedural Generation
videos.description: In an industry where procedural generation systems are historically programmer-led, Red Hook Studios took an unconventional approach and crafted a system that prioritized the game's art direction instead. videos.title: Rendering 'Assassin's Creed Shadows'
videos.zhTitle: Rendering 'Assassin's Creed Shadows'
videos.description: Assassin's Creed Shadows is a large systemic open world game with dynamic time of day cycles, a systemic weather system, and seasons. Such a large world and dynamic setting poses many rendering challenges. This GDC 2025 talk focuses on a typical frame and walks the audience through the various steps required to produce the final render, focusing on gpu driven pipeline, lighting improvements and other new developments

videos.title: Training AI to Solve Puzzles in Little Learning Machines
videos.zhTitle: Training AI to Solve Puzzles in Little Learning Machines
videos.description: Little Learning Machines is a puzzle game with a fairly unique premise: rather than solving the puzzles yourself, you train non-player characters called Animos to solve them for you. clips.zhTitle: AI and Games / MLMove: The LLM Trained to Play Counter-Strike | AI and Games #81
clips.zhSummary: 来自 AI and Games 的《MLMove: The LLM Trained to Play Counter-Strike | AI and Games #81》章节「MLMove: The LLM Trained to Play Counter-Strike | AI and Games #81」。 For this episode of @AIandGames, Tommy sits down with David Durst: a Stanford PhD graduate to discuss his research: the 'language model of movement'.  A transformer (like those used in LLMs) that has learned to move teams of bots like humans in Counter-Strike.  By training against a dataset of retakes on Dust 2, MLMove hugs walls for cover, covers sightlines and coordinates strategy like a regular human player.

clips.zhTitle: GDC / Evolving Worlds from Chaos: The Art-Led Approach of 'Darkest Dungeon 2's' Procedural Generation
clips.zhSummary: 来自 GDC 的《Evolving Worlds from Chaos: The Art-Led Approach of 'Darkest Dungeon 2's' Procedural Generation》章节「Evolving Worlds from Chaos: The Art-Led Approach of 'Darkest Dungeon 2's' Procedural Generation」。 In an industry where procedural generation systems are historically programmer-led, Red Hook Studios took an unconventional approach and crafted a system that prioritized the game's art direction instead.
